text: It is not too much to expect that our children will enjoy in their homes electrical energy too cheap to meter.
author: Lewis Strauss
source: Speech to National Association of Science Writers, 1954

didItHoldUp.analysis: The chairman of the Atomic Energy Commission promised electricity so cheap we wouldn't bother measuring it. Nuclear power turned out to be expensive, complex, and politically divisive. The phrase 'too cheap to meter' became shorthand for technological overpromising. But here's an interesting twist: solar energy in the 2020s is approaching costs so low that the metering infrastructure may cost more than the electricity. Strauss might get his wish after all — just from a different source, seven decades late.
